subject: Physical Education

lesson.title: 1.11 SPORTS BIOMECHANICS
topics: Sports Biomechanics is the study of how the human body moves during sports and exercise.
It uses ideas from science and physics to help athletes and coaches/sports teachers understand
movements like running, jumping, kicking and throwing.
This knowledge helps improve performance, develop better techniques and prevent injuries. For
activities: as Natural (playing grounds…), Artificial (Modified classroom…) and Technology (video clips,
Balls, surfaces with different textures, Spring balance, small weights, calculators, stop watch,
measuring tape/meter ruler, cones or markers, whistle, (Locally improvised materials; grass,
sand, smooth floor, plastic bottles, carpet)…
Observe how friction affects movement of the ball on smooth and rough surfaces
assessment: z time management (complete tasks within the given time)
z understanding of concepts (reflecting knowledge of concepts)
z Relevance (answers directly addressing the question
